expected_properties: After a rising edge, q[7] reflects the driven value of d and all other bits shift right by one position.
additional_cases.goal: Validate that a single clock edge correctly updates q with the shift operation and the new most‑significant bit.
notes: Uses conservative observation; no exact cycle latency is assumed beyond the rising edge.
preconditions: The register q is initialized to 0 (via the initial block).
stimulus_intent: Apply a single value on d and observe q after the next rising edge of clk.
timing_assumptions: Observe q after one rising edge of clk.

expected_properties: A change on d immediately before a rising edge is captured as the new most‑significant bit of q.
additional_cases.goal: Check that input transitions close to the clock edge are still reflected in q after the edge, under conservative timing assumptions.
notes: Conservative: assume synchronous capture on the rising edge without modeling setup/hold windows.
stimulus_intent: Toggle d just before the rising edge of clk and verify q[7] after the edge.
timing_assumptions: Assume the design samples d on the rising edge of clk.

expected_properties: A legal operation (single clock edge) causes q to update with the shifted value and new MSB from d.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Apply one legal input on d, advance one rising edge of clk, and observe the stable post‑operation value of q.
notes: Conservative observation: do not assume any hidden latency beyond the visible clock edge.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative value on d (e.g., 1) and capture q after the next rising edge of clk.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations; observe q after the rising edge of clk.
